---
title: "The Environment of: Wastebacteria"
description: |
 Listen to Trace Martin distill the wonders of our wastewater treatment system!
image: wastewater_ep.png
date: 02-11-2026
format:
  html:
    lightbox: TRUE
---
<iframe data-testid="embed-iframe" style="border-radius:12px" src="https://open.spotify.com/embed/episode/53vj9tIH8RbnrkaQ4S5N47?utm_source=generator&t=0" width="100%" height="352" frameBorder="0" allowfullscreen="" allow="autoplay; clipboard-write; encrypted-media; fullscreen; picture-in-picture" loading="lazy"></iframe>

In this episode, host Kylie Keenan sits down with Trace Martin, a fourth-year PhD student at the Bren school studying wastewater treatment, coastal water quality, and fecal indicator bacteria. 

Trace is also - unofficially but unanimously - the best TA ever!

Throughout the episode Trace shares his unconventional path from Santa Barbara City College to UCSB as a Geography major before becoming the youngest lab manager in the City of Santa Barbara’s wastewater lab and ultimately pursuing a PhD that bridges hands-on lab work with academic research. From microbial ecosystems inside wastewater plants, to the regulatory hurdles of approving new testing methods, this episode demystifies an often invisible system that impacts everyone. 

Tune in for a fascinating look at the science behind wastewater and the people working behind the scenes to treat it.

### Show Notes
Guest: Trace Martin 

Our Instagram: @envionmentofitall
[Our LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/in/theenvironmentofitall/)

### Credits: 
Kylie Keenan - Host 
Ian Del Tredici - Producer | Writer
Leila Zavala - Assistant Producer | Social Media
Malie Quinn - Audio Engineer & Editor 
Cara Sandford - Writer
Taylor Platz - Outreach Coordinator
Jasmine Schwam - Executive Producer

Original Music by Maile Quinn 


### Special Thanks: 
Alexandra Phillips 
Joanna Tang 
UCSB Science Communication Club 

Sponsored by: The Green Initiative Fund (TGIF)
